questions.question: What determines the permissions that Notion Agent has when performing actions?

options.text: The permissions set by the workspace admin for the Agent itself
options.explanation: Agents do not have separate permissions; they inherit from the user.

options.text: The permissions of the user who is chatting with the Agent
options.explanation: Agent has the same permissions as you, so it can only view or edit what you can.

options.text: A special set of AI‑only permissions granted by Notion
options.explanation: There is no distinct AI permission layer.

options.text: The default read‑only permissions for all Notion AI features
options.explanation: Agent can edit as well, provided you have edit rights.

questions.question: Which of the following actions can you NOT ask Notion Agent to perform?

options.text: Create a new page based on a style guide
options.explanation: Agent can create and edit pages.

options.text: Summarize a CSV file you uploaded
options.explanation: Agent can ingest CSVs and summarize them.

options.text: Change the permission level of a shared page
options.explanation: Agent cannot modify page permissions.

options.text: Generate a list of action items from the current page
options.explanation: Generating action items is a supported use case.

questions.question: How can you provide additional context to Notion Agent beyond the page you’re currently viewing?

options.text: By @‑mentioning another page or person in the query
options.explanation: Using @ lets Agent focus on the mentioned resource.

options.text: By selecting "All sources" and picking specific sources in the chat
options.explanation: All sources lets you add multiple pages, databases, etc.

options.text: By uploading a file (e.g., PDF or CSV) for Agent to read
options.explanation: Uploaded files become part of the context.

options.text: All of the above
options.explanation: Each method adds context, and you can combine them.

questions.question: What happens when you switch Notion Agent to the "Auto" model?

options.text: Agent will only use information from the web and ignore your workspace data
options.explanation: The Auto model may limit access to workspace content, relying on web sources.

options.text: Agent becomes faster but loses the ability to edit pages
options.explanation: Speed isn’t the primary change; edit capability remains if workspace access is allowed.

options.text: Agent will automatically create a new database for you
options.explanation: Switching models doesn’t trigger automatic actions.

options.text: Agent will start generating meeting notes automatically
options.explanation: Meeting notes are not a built‑in feature of the Auto model.

questions.question: Which of the following is a correct way to personalize Notion Agent’s behavior?

options.text: Give the Agent a new name and select accessories in the personalization menu
options.explanation: Naming and accessories are part of personalization.

options.text: Create a private page called "My Notion AI" and add instructions there
options.explanation: A private page can hold custom instructions.

options.text: Share the instructions page with the whole workspace so anyone can edit it
options.explanation: While possible, changes by others affect behavior; best practice is to copy the page.

options.text: All of the above
options.explanation: A, B, and D (except sharing broadly) are ways to personalize; however, sharing broadly is not recommended, making D partially correct. The safest answer is A.

questions.question: What can you do if you want to view your recent conversations with Notion Agent?

options.text: Hover over Notion AI in the sidebar, click the clock icon, and search the history
options.explanation: That is the described method for accessing the last 30 days of chats.

options.text: Open the Settings menu and select "Chat Archive"
options.explanation: There is no Settings‑based archive feature.

options.text: Use the command "/history" in a new chat window
options.explanation: No such slash command exists for history.

options.text: All of the above
options.explanation: Only option A is correct.

questions.question: Which limitation applies to Notion Agent when working with video content?

options.text: Agent can extract transcripts from YouTube videos automatically
options.explanation: Agent cannot process non‑PDF embeds like video transcripts.

options.text: Agent cannot answer questions that rely on content from a video transcript embedded in a page
options.explanation: The article states Agent can’t use content from non‑PDF embeds such as video transcripts.

options.text: Agent can summarize the audio of an uploaded MP3 file
options.explanation: Only PDFs and CSVs are mentioned for file ingestion.

options.text: Agent can create a new video embed based on a text prompt
options.explanation: Creating video embeds is not listed among capabilities.

questions.question: If you need a Notion Agent to create a complex database property like a rollup or formula, what is the correct approach?

options.text: Ask the Agent to add the rollup or formula directly; it will create it for you
options.explanation: Agent cannot create or edit advanced properties like formulas or rollups.

options.text: Create the basic property yourself and then ask the Agent to suggest the formula syntax
options.explanation: While Agent can help suggest syntax, it cannot create the advanced property itself.

options.text: Use the "Auto" model, which supports advanced property creation
options.explanation: Model switching does not grant ability to create advanced properties.

options.text: Upload a CSV file containing the desired formula, and Agent will import it
options.explanation: CSV ingestion does not translate into database formulas.

questions.question: How does providing thumbs‑down feedback differ from thumbs‑up feedback for Notion Agent?

options.text: Thumbs‑down feedback is used to train the Agent’s model directly
options.explanation: Feedback is not used to train the model.

options.text: Thumbs‑down allows you to optionally give a reason for the feedback
options.explanation: When you give a thumbs‑down you can also explain why.

options.text: Thumbs‑up feedback automatically saves the Agent’s response as a template
options.explanation: Saving as a template is not mentioned.

options.text: Both thumbs‑up and thumbs‑down feedback are ignored by Notion
options.explanation: Feedback is shared with the Notion team for improvements.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes the process to change the visual placement of the Notion Agent chat window?

options.text: Select "Switch chat mode" and choose either Sidebar or Floating
options.explanation: The article explains this exact step.

options.text: Drag the chat window to any location on the screen
options.explanation: Chat placement is changed via the Switch chat mode option, not free dragging.

options.text: Open Settings → Appearance → Chat Position
options.explanation: No such Settings path exists for chat placement.

options.text: Use a keyboard shortcut Ctrl+Shift+C to toggle chat mode
options.explanation: No keyboard shortcut is described for this purpose.
